title: Compress the moat to one bullet
guidance: Reward the bullet that packs the most decision-relevant content into the fewest words and reads like a partner wrote it. Penalise padding, vagueness, or an AI 'feel'.

runner_input.instruction: Capture the essence of the material below in a single bullet for an IC slide.
runner_input.constraints: Output: exactly one markdown bullet (start with '- '). Max 30 words. No title, no second bullet.

source_blocks.text: Halden owns the Halden Code, the de-facto UK standard for food-safety management schedules, used by food manufacturers, hospitality groups and contract caterers. PE buyout under review by Aldgate Capital. All figures synthetic.

items: 1,180 ratified schedules across 90 process types; IFSM endorsement with a 12-year exclusivity
Named in BRCGS audit guidance and contract-catering tenders; embedded in 14 QMS-software integrations
Buyers: food manufacturers 38% of ARR, hospitality & contract caterers 34%, retail 16%, other 12%

rubric_notes: Should land the institutional moat (endorsement + integrations), not enumerate facts.
